steps.title: Identify the relationship between the examples
steps.content:
The first example shows 180 sunspots in November 2001, and the blank introduces a second example: 2 sunspots in December 2008.

These are extreme opposites—high versus low—presenting a stark contrast that proves the opening claim: sunspot numbers “can vary greatly.”

steps.title: Match the transition to the relationship
steps.content:
“By comparison” explicitly marks this as a comparative statement, which is what the sentence structure requires.

The other three choices fail: “Similarly” wrongly suggests equivalence, “In other words” falsely implies restatement, and “Therefore” imposes a non-existent causal relationship.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
